qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWE DEFLATE decompression lacks size limits enabling denial-of-service via zip bomb
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package but different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets Authlib JWE DEFLATE decompression without size limits, while the advisory targets JWS/JWT signature verification bypass for alg:none with a blank signature.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWT introspection endpoint skips audience validation when resource_server is unset
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package and JWT domain but different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns RFC9068 introspection audience validation with resource_server=None, not jwt.decode accepting an unsigned alg:none token after signature verification should fail.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in the run manifest identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is Authlib accepting a forged JWT with alg:none and an empty signature during signature verification, causing authentication or authorization bypass. The manifest-backed qualifying findings cover Authlib JWE DEFLATE decompression DoS and Authlib RFC9068 audience validation bypass.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7wc2-qxgw-g8gg describes authlib jwt.decode accepting a malicious JWT header {'alg': 'none'} with a blank signature in versions 1.6.5 through 1.6.6.
jwe_deflate_zip_bomb/vulnerability_report.json describes authlib/jose/rfc7518/jwe_zips.py unbounded zlib.decompress in JWE DEFLATE handling, not alg:none JWS verification.

jwt_introspection_audience_bypass/vulnerability_report.json describes JWTIntrospectionEndpoint and JWTBearerTokenValidator skipping audience comparison when resource_server is None, not accepting unsigned JWTs.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
